title: Talisman of the Raven
content:

{{Otheruses|Raven}}

{{Quote|One of our sisters has yet to return the Talisman of the Raven.|Clan Mother Talzin|<ref name="BoSSotDS" />}}
The '''Talisman of the Raven''' was a [[Ring/Legends|ring]] used by the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] of [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] as a [[Talisman of Transformation]] that allowed the user to [[Metamorphosis|assume the form]] of a [[Raven/Legends|raven]]. According to [[Mother (title)/Legends|Mother]] [[Talzin/Legends|Talzin]], who led the united clans of Nightsisters during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Talisman would tap into the prime raven [[Spirit (Nightsisters)|spirit]] in the spirit world and lend its powers to the user. Originally held under the custodianship of a [[Shaman of the Nightsisters]], it was stolen by [[Sister (Witches of Dathomir)|Sister]] [[Charal]], one of the Dathomiri [[Witch/Legends|witches]]. She fled to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] and never returned the Talisman to the [[Coven/Legends|coven]].

Charal used it to great effect during her service to the [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] Marauders, a band of [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. However, the Talisman was later seized by the Marauders' leader, [[King/Legends|King]] [[Terak]], who wore it around his neck. Eventually, the ring was destroyed in an [[Battle of Endor (Ewok–Sanyassan conflict)|encounter]] with the moon's native [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. The great power of the ring was unleashed upon Terak, burning him to [[Death/Legends|death]]. As Charal was flying around in raven form during the fight, the untimely destruction of the Talisman trapped her in this shape with no hope of escape.

==Properties==
{{Quote|Talismans of Transformation allow the wearer to change shape in the physical realm, to become an animal and tap into the animal's spirit essence, for example.|Clan Mother Talzin|<ref name="BoSSotDS" />}}
The Talisman of the Raven allowed its user to [[Metamorphosis|assume the shape]] of a [[Raven/Legends|raven]]. Such pieces of arcane technology were created by the [[Witches of Dathomir]], a society of [[Gender/Legends|all-female]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]]. Both [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemists]] and [[Lore Keeper|Jedi scholars]] could not fully explain how the artifacts worked and unsuccessfully tried to replicate their effects. A [[Talisman of Transformation]],<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> the Talisman of the Raven consisted of a [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Gemstone/Legends|gemstone]] set in a [[Gold/Legends|golden]] [[Ring/Legends|ring]].<ref name="TBFE" />

On the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], a clan of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Witch/Legends|witches]] known as the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] would create such artifacts by [[Force artifact/Legends|empowering]] receptacles of various types with the power of what they called "the [[Spirit (Nightsisters)|spirits]]"—their way of describing the work of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. According to [[Talzin/Legends|Talzin]], [[Mother (title)/Legends|Clan Mother]] and [[Shaman of the Nightsisters|Shaman]] of the united Nightsister [[Coven/Legends|covens]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the realm of the spirits was populated by the life-energies of beings that corresponded to real animals in the mundane realm, and was ruled by the universal manifestations of male and female energy. She believed every existing [[Species/Legends|species]] to have a correspondent representative ur-spirit in the spirit realm—for example, all the [[Ssurian/Legends|ssurians]] of the material plane would have a single corresponding "ur-ssurian" spirit in the immaterial realm. Talismans of Transformation were believed to allow the wearer to turn into a specific creature by tapping into its ur-spirit's energy.<ref name="BoSSotDS" />

[[File:Crowshapeshift.jpg|thumb|left|240px|The Talisman of the Raven at work]]
The Talisman of the Raven had been impregnated with the energy of the raven's ur-spirit, granting its wearer the power to take the shape of the black [[Bird/Legends|bird]].<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> In order to draw on the ur-raven's power, the wearer needed to give the stone a twist.<ref name="TBFE" /> That effect could also be achieved accidentally by the impact of a blow imparted to the ring.<ref name="E:SOE">''[[Star Wars: Ewoks—Shadows of Endor]]''</ref> If the Talisman was lost or destroyed while the user was still subsumed by the ur-raven, the user would be trapped in raven form.<ref name="dbchar">{{DB|character|charal|Charal}}</ref>

As with all other [[Magic/Legends|magical]] artifacts, the Talisman of the Raven belonged to the community of the [[Sister (Witches of Dathomir)|Sisterhood]]. According to Talzin, the keeping of the talismans was the responsibility of the Nightsister [[Shaman/Legends|Shaman]]—an elder witch whose role was to interpret the will of the spirits. Any Sister who wanted to use the ring was required to borrow it from the Shaman, and was expected to return it to the coven afterward. In ''[[Wild Power]]'', her main [[Book/Legends|manifesto]] on Nightsister philosophy, practice, and role in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Talzin complained about a Sister—[[Charal]]—never having returned the Talisman of the Raven.<ref name="BoSSotDS" />

==History==
{{Quote|You may have the ring back only when you find me the magic crystal.|Terak, seizing the Talisman from Charal|<ref name="RWC">''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Charal_ring.png|thumb|right|Charal using the Talisman of the Raven]]
The earliest known owner of the Talisman was the Shaman of the Nightsisters who served during the first century before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> Sometime before [[96 BBY]], Sister Charal, a member of the clan, managed to escape Dathomir,<ref name="CSWE" /> taking the Talisman, on lease from the Shaman, with her.<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> Pursued by her fellow witches, Charal traveled from [[Star system/Legends|star system]] to [[Star/Legends|star]] system with the stolen ring. She joined with a band of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], the [[Sanyassan Marauders]] from the nearby planet [[Sanyassa IV|Sanyassa]], led by [[King/Legends|King]] [[Terak]]. Together, they were eventually stranded on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], a distant [[Moon/Legends|moon]] untouched by [[Galactic community|galactic civilization]],<ref name="dbchar" /> where many intergalactic travelers had found themselves marooned due to the stellar anomalies surrounding it, such as the [[Endor Gate/Legends|Endor Gate]] [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]].<ref name="EndorModdell">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}}</ref><ref name="Castaways">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref> Unlike many other castaways, the [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassans]] were aggressive, and they desperately sought to escape the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] moon, scavenging whatever technology they could find. Charal used the ring to great effect during that time,<ref name="CSWE" /> intimidating the Marauders with displays of its power, and eventually becoming Terak's partner.<ref name="FF10">{{FactFile|10|END 1-4|The Forest Moon of Endor}}</ref>

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Charal became a victim of her own magic ring during a showdown with [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] of a [[Bright Tree Village tribe|tribe]] of [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] inhabiting in [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], and his young apprentice [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]]. While she was fighting the two Ewoks, Charal's talisman was struck by Paploo's [[Slingshot/Legends|slingshot]], which activated the artifact's powers in the heat of battle. The witch then made a strategic retreat, electing to take her revenge another time.<ref name="E:SOE" /> A little later, in 3.5 ABY,<ref name="SWBTM" /> a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]] named [[Cindel Towani]] crashed on Endor with her [[Towani family|family]]. Charal and the Marauders saw a chance to escape their place of forced exile in the [[Towani family star cruiser|family's starship]]. The Sanyassans believed that ship's [[crystal oscillator]] held the "power" that would allow them to return home. Cindel's [[Parent/Legends|parents]] and [[Mace Towani|older brother]] were [[Death/Legends|murdered]] by Terak and Charal, and the little girl was captured. When Charal was found unable to "conjure" the power of the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]], she was put in [[Prison/Legends|jail]] along with Towani and had her ring confiscated. A young Ewok warrior named [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] teamed up with [[Noa Briqualon|his]] [[Teek (Teek)|friends]] to rescue Towani from [[Terak's Keep]], and they ran off with the crystal oscillator and headed to their own [[Noa Briqualon's starship|crashed ship]].<ref name="TBFE" />

[[File:Terak_death.png|thumb|left|150px|The destruction of the ring burns King Terak to death.]]
King Terak, intent on revenge, decided to carry out a punitive expedition. He ordered his minions to recapture the crystal oscillator. He allowed Charal to use the ring, but took it back immediately after her transformation—he wanted to keep Charal in bird form so she would track the group without betraying him and running away. When the Marauders found their targets, a [[Battle of Endor (Ewok–Sanyassan conflict)|pitched battle]] between the two groups ensued. Terak fought with the Talisman of the Raven in a pendant around his neck. During the melee, a rock slung from Wicket's slingshot hit the ring, breaking the Talisman and releasing its power. The ring shattered and pieces of the magic stone were driven into Terak's [[Breast/Legends|chest]]. Terak clutched at his [[Heart/Legends|heart]], and he burned to death on exposure with the ring's "spirit" energy.<ref name="TBFE" /> According to the shamanistic Dathomiri beliefs, the raven ur-spirit that was trapped in the ring returned to the spirit realm at that moment.<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> Because the transformation spell was still active when the ring was destroyed, Charal was doomed to remain a raven forever.<ref name="dbchar" /> The transformed witch was last seen dropping down to the ground in the hope of reclaiming her broken ring; then she flew off into the woods.<ref name="FF123">{{FactFile|123|TOW 1-4|Cindel Towani}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Talisman of Transformation: This lets people turn into animals and references the 1985 TV movie ''Ewoks: The Battle for Endor'', specifically the magic ring worn by the evil witch Charal (which allowed her to turn into a raven).|Daniel Wallace, in his endnotes for ''Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side''|<ref name="BOS endnotes" />}}
The Talisman of the Raven first appeared in the [[1985]] made-for-TV film ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''. While often seen on-screen, it was never referred to in dialogue.<ref name="TBFE" />

While early sources implied Charal's ring could turn its wearer into virtually anything,<ref name="AGSWU2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> [[Daniel Wallace]]'s ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' stated that Nightsister Talismans of Transformation have limited powers and can only transform people into one specific creature.<ref name="BoSSotDS" /> In his endnotes for the ''Book of Sith'', Wallace revealed that the Talismans of Transformations had been specifically created with Charal and her magic ring in mind.<ref name="BOS endnotes">{{Blogspot|subdomain=geekosity|url=2012/04/endnotes-for-star-wars-book-of-sith.html|name=Daniel Wallace's Geekosity|text=Endnotes for Star Wars: Book of Sith (part 4)}}</ref>

There is a conflict between several sources regarding the death of King Terak. While Terak's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry on [[StarWars.com]] stated that he was burned by the power of the ring,<ref name="DBTer">{{DB|character|terak|Terak}}</ref> other sources like the children's book ''[[The Ring, the Witch, and the Crystal: An Ewok Adventure]]'' stated the Sanyassan King was turned to stone.<ref name="RWC" /> This article follows the Databank's version of the facts, as it was once the most official ''[[Star Wars]]'' database.
